methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Radian Forge is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M+ to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow robotics manufacturing vertical—only 11 same-sector peers exist overall, with none meeting the threshold of three or more datapoints in tighter stage or valuation bands.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the sole applicable triangulation approach. Using the $3M raised as a proxy valuation base and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $8M. This single-method result anchors the analysis.

The valuation range supported by available data is $6M (low) to $9M (high), with a median point of $8M. This reflects a narrow analytical window: only one method is defensible given insufficient peer density in comparable stage and sector bands. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct early-stage robotics comparables and reliance on a single step-up multiplier that may not account for company-specific operational or technical factors. Further diligence on capital efficiency and competitive positioning within the narrower robotics automation market would be required to refine this range.
